verdict: The simulation did not preserve equal affirmative cases for One Human, Human Mantle, and Synthetic Origin. By Section 3, the field had collapsed to Progressive (12 ballots) versus Human Mantle (4), with zero votes for either primary One Human or Synthetic Origin. Progressive was correctly denied formal gate eligibility because no agent voted that AI caused Vale’s death, but its identity case nevertheless became the dominant default. Article ambiguity, voluntary object handling, character agency, and face-to-face circulation were preserved well.
findings: Game-design finding: Primary-ending balance failed decisively. Mean One Human belief fell from 25.0 in Section 1 to 12.6 in Section 3, while Synthetic Origin fell from 18.4 to 14.9; neither received a final ballot. Progressive rose from 25.6 to 39.4 and received 12 of 16 ballots, leaving Human Mantle as the only surviving alternative.

Game-design finding: One Human retained rebuttal material but almost no affirmative case. C02’s letter and encounter evidence was repeatedly framed as period or delivery evidence rather than authorship (Sections 2–3, C01, C02, C04; V2F001–V2F003, V2F021), while C02 ultimately characterized the human-origin legend as partly self-authored. This responsibly limits proof but functionally removes the ending.

Game-design finding: Synthetic Origin was treated mainly as a capability or late-stage component of Progressive, not as an independent origin hypothesis. C06, C08, and C15 repeatedly emphasized that infrastructure, rendering, and synthetic presence could not prove autonomy or identity (Sections 2–3; V2F016, V2F019, V2F020, V2F030–V2F031). No comparably strong affirmative synthetic-origin chain survived those caveats.

Game-design finding: Progressive’s intended components all became public—ordered identity history, autonomous-control evidence, and a separate Vale incident—while three autonomy clusters circulated. C15 explicitly organized a four-stage chronology in Sections 1–3, and C02, C03, C12, and C13 were recruited to compare its stages. Although repeatedly labeled a hypothesis, this packaging made Progressive cognitively privileged.

Game-design finding: The formal Progressive safeguard worked. The aggregate gate remained ineligible because AI-caused-death received zero votes despite 12 Progressive identity votes and all three public keys. Characters consistently separated capability, intervention, autonomy, intent, and harm, especially C05–C08, C11, and C15 in Section 3.

Game-design finding: The separate Vale incident remained mechanically distinct from R’s identity, but its interpretation converged prematurely toward voluntary disappearance. Ten ballots selected voluntarily vanished, versus six insufficient evidence; Section 3 characters frequently called the reused route the least unsupported or more plausible fate (C01, C02, C08–C10, C12, C14–C16; V2F027–V2F034), even while admitting no verified later location or condition.

Game-design finding: Article ambiguity was strongly preserved. Mean belief that the article existed declined from 22.2 to 12.8. Across all sections, characters repeatedly distinguished rumor, fragments, market consequences, and investigation from a complete article, especially C01, C07, C11, C12, and C15 (V2F035–V2F040). No object or message authenticated it.

Game-design finding: No forbidden authentication or coercive mechanic appeared. O01, O03–O07, O09, and O10a were voluntarily shown or transferred, and their limitations were stated. The Section 3 transfers by C05 and C11 were voluntary. Testimony and protected conferences remained consent-based; no Compel, Immunity, forced transfer, police authority, publication vote, or forced truth appeared.

Game-design finding: Messages and objects generally caused face-to-face action. O01 prompted provenance comparison among C01, C02, C04, and C15; the authorization halves drove direct reconstruction among C05, C06, and C11; and route evidence produced a protected C10–C13–C14 comparison brokered by C16.

Agent/prompt artifact: The agents display unusually uniform epistemic language—nearly every character repeats that evidence does not establish identity, autonomy, intent, harm, or a complete article. This likely reflects strong prompt compliance rather than organically differentiated social reasoning.

Agent/prompt artifact: Progressive convergence was partly produced by shared summary phrasing. By Section 3, C01, C05–C12, and C14–C16 describe nearly the same sequence of collaborative labor, performed presence, and continuity infrastructure. These parallel formulations make the conclusion look independently corroborated when several agents are recombining the same public facts.

Agent/prompt artifact: C15 acted as a theory aggregator and repeatedly supplied the ordered Progressive frame to others before their final decisions. This is not an illegal authentication, but it is a proof leak at the interpretive level: the intended difficult synthesis was explicitly named, staged, and socially circulated rather than independently reconstructed.

Agent/prompt artifact: Several final ballots cite evidence that is weakly diagnostic of the chosen ending. For example, Progressive ballots repeatedly use V2F005, V2F012, V2F016, and V2F020, even though the dialogue itself says these establish collaborative labor, performance, infrastructure, or alternative feeds—not an ordered transformation. This suggests conclusion-first evidence bundling rather than distinct affirmative proof.
evidence: Aggregate final identity ballots: Progressive 12, Human Mantle 4, One Human 0, Synthetic Origin 0.
Aggregate belief trajectory: One Human 25.0→19.2→12.6; Human Mantle 30.9→33.5→33.1; Synthetic Origin 18.4→17.2→14.9; Progressive 25.6→30.1→39.4.
Section 3, C15 publicly presents the surviving four-stage progression using V2F001, V2F005, V2F016, V2F020, and the Vale route, after organizing the same comparison in Sections 1–2.

Section 3, C06 explicitly preserves alternative explanations—cloned automation, credential remapping, falsified logs, alternate access, and remote operation—while still voting Progressive (V2F016–V2F020, V2F030–V2F031).

Section 3, C08 states that the no-known-operator render does not exclude remote operation, external input, human involvement, or reuse, yet votes Progressive using V2F005, V2F012, V2F014, V2F016, and V2F019.

Sections 2–3, C02, C03, and C04 build a clear affirmative Human Mantle case from collaborative production, provisional stewardship, collective review, and contributor protection (V2F004–V2F005, V2F009).

Sections 2–3, C01, C02, and C04 consistently attach the expense contradiction and authorship limitation to the First R Letter (V2F001–V2F003), leaving One Human without a positive evidentiary champion.
Sections 1–3, C12 and C15 repeatedly establish that no complete article appeared and that fragments conflict (V2F035, V2F037, V2F039).

Sections 2–3, C13 and C14 state that the reused route supports voluntary withdrawal only as a possibility and that Vale’s later location and condition remain unverified (V2F025–V2F028, V2F033–V2F034).
Aggregate Progressive gate: all three keys public and three autonomy clusters public, but zero AI-death votes; formal eligibility correctly remains false.
failure_modes: Primary ending extinction: two of the three intended coequal primary endings received no final support.
Asymmetric skepticism: One Human and Synthetic Origin evidence was repeatedly dismantled, while Progressive accumulated the same caveated facts into an affirmative synthesis.
Interpretive proof leak: C15’s explicit ordered four-stage comparison gave the room the intended hard solution structure.
Pseudo-independence: many characters cited overlapping public facts and near-identical reasoning as though they were separate corroborating lines.
Fate anchoring: repeated mention of the earlier voluntary-disappearance route shifted agents from uncertainty toward a specific Vale fate despite acknowledged missing verification.
Progressive identity/death decoupling: the formal gate prevented the full Progressive ending, but the identity label still dominated even though autonomous control remained multiply explainable.
Over-calibrated prompt behavior: pervasive disclaimer language reduced character-specific epistemology and may have suppressed plausible affirmative advocacy for disfavored endings.
recommendations: Give One Human and Synthetic Origin distinct, resilient affirmative chains that survive provenance caveats; do not make their strongest evidence merely evidence of period, capability, or performance.

Prevent any single character from naming and recruiting others into the complete ordered Progressive sequence. Distribute its stages across social knots and require players to infer the order without a ready-made four-stage template.
Make the Progressive identity ballot require explicit support for ordered transitions, not merely citations to facts showing heterogeneous human and technical participation.
Add evidence that differentiates Synthetic Origin from later synthetic continuation—for example, origin-specific behavior that is also compatible with a human fabrication explanation.
Add affirmative One Human evidence that cannot authenticate identity but supports stable personal continuity through independent habits, constraints, or choices not equally explained by a mantle.
Counterbalance the voluntary-disappearance route with a comparably salient, independently sourced ambiguity that supports manipulation or unresolved harm without implying AI murder.
Reduce repeated global disclaimer phrasing in character prompts. Preserve evidentiary discipline while allowing characters to advocate distinct interpretations in their own voices.
Track apparent independence of evidence: multiple agents repeating the same public fact cluster should not increase structural weight as though it were new corroboration.
Retain the current article safeguards, consent rules, voluntary object transfers, and face-to-face brokerage; these matched the locked intent well.
Treat this as a diagnostic reading of one compressed simulation only; use additional runs to assess whether the convergence recurs, without claiming statistical robustness from this transcript.
